diffCoverage.notes: Checked what crosses the IPC boundary on the inbound event path and what the live smoke prints.

findings.evidence: start_listening validates the credential payload at the command boundary, confines credentials/session to the zalo layer, and emits only IncomingMessage (no tokens) over zalo://message. The live smoke output was scanned: it contains no imei/cookie/userAgent values and no recipient phone number.
findings.fix: When session-manager persists sessions, keep emitting only non-secret DTOs and never log message content at info level by default.

findings.evidence: IncomingMessage models untrusted server data as plain typed fields with optional text; non-text content maps to None rather than executing or interpolating server strings.
findings.fix: Continue treating listener payloads as untrusted as more event kinds are mapped.

summary: Security review of the realtime inbound path: credentials stay in the zalo layer, only non-secret IncomingMessage DTOs cross IPC, and the attested live-smoke sidecar was verified free of credentials and the recipient phone number. Server event data is treated as untrusted typed data. No blocking findings.
